2603.19017.title: What Really Controls Temporal Reasoning in Large Language Models: Tokenisation or Representation of Time?
2603.19017.paper_summary: We present MultiTempBench, a multilingual temporal reasoning benchmark spanning three tasks, date arithmetic, time zone conversion, and temporal relation extraction across five languages (English, German, Chinese, Arabic, and Hausa) and multiple calendar conventions (Gregorian, Hijri, and Chinese Lunar). MultiTempBench contains $15,000$ examples built by translating $750$ curated English questions and expanding each into controlled date-format variants. We evaluate 20 LLMs and introduce the multilingual Date Fragmentation Ratio (mDFR), calibrated with human severity ratings, together with geometric-probing analyses of internal temporal representations. We find tokenisation quality of temporal artefacts is a resource-dependent bottleneck: in low-resource languages and rarer calendar formats, fragmentation disrupts Year/Month/Day separation and accuracy collapses, while high-resource settings are often robust to digit-level splitting. Beyond tokenisation, crossed mixed-effects regression shows that temporal linearity is the strongest predictor of temporal reasoning in high-resource languages, whereas fragmentation is the stronger predictor in low-resource languages. Code is available at: https://github.com/gagan3012/mtb
2603.19017.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了 MultiTempBench，一個多語言的時間推理基準，涵蓋三個任務：日期運算、時區轉換和時間關係提取，涉及五種語言（英語、德語、中文、阿拉伯語和豪薩語）以及多種日曆慣例（公曆、伊斯蘭曆和中國農曆）。MultiTempBench 包含 $15,000$ 個範例，這些範例是通過翻譯 $750$ 個精選的英語問題並將每個問題擴展為受控的日期格式變體來構建的。我們評估了 20 個大型語言模型（LLMs），並引入了多語言日期碎片化比率（mDFR），該比率經過人類嚴重性評分的校準，並結合了對內部時間表示的幾何探測分析。我們發現時間文物的標記質量是一個資源依賴的瓶頸：在低資源語言和較少見的日曆格式中，碎片化會干擾年/月/日的分離，導致準確性崩潰，而在高資源環境中，通常對數字級的分割具有穩健性。除了標記之外，交叉混合效應回歸顯示，在高資源語言中，時間線性是時間推理的最強預測因子，而在低資源語言中，碎片化則是更強的預測因子。代碼可在以下鏈接獲得：https://github.com/gagan3012/mtb
2603.19017.author: Gagan Bhatia et.al.
2603.19017.authors: Gagan Bhatia, Ahmad Muhammad Isa, Maxime Peyrard, Wei Zhao
